relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Divine speaks the covenant words.
The first word is liberation: I brought you out from the house of slavery.
No rival power stands before the face of YHWH.
Do not reduce the Living One into carved form.
Do not bow to images; distorted allegiance echoes through generations.
Loyal love reaches to thousands who love and keep.
Do not carry the Name into emptiness.
Remember Sabbath and make it holy.
Six days hold ordinary work.
The seventh day gives rest to household, servants, animals, and sojourners.
Sabbath mirrors Creator rest.
Honor father and mother for long life on the ground.
Do not murder.
Do not betray covenant intimacy.
Do not steal.
Do not bear false witness against your companion.
Do not let desire become grasping toward your companion's life.
The people see thunder, fire, horn, and smoke, and tremble.
They ask Moses to mediate the overwhelming voice.
Moses says reverence, not panic, is the point.
Moses approaches the thick darkness where Divine is.
YHWH reminds them: you saw I spoke from heavens.
Do not make silver or gold gods beside me.
Earth altar remembers the Name and receives blessing.
Uncut stones keep worship simple and unpossessed.
Even approach to altar is guarded from exposure and shame.

contemplative_note: Exodus 20 is not law before love; it is love after liberation: the freed people learn worship, rest, truth, fidelity, restraint, and reverent desire.
